sutta: once the verbal uttara dwelt near mahi savatu on the sankey yaka mountain in dava jalika there the venable uttara addressed the monk saying proven says well it is for a monk to review from time to time his own faults well it is for him to reveal the faults of another well it is to review his own attainments well it is to review another's now at that time the royal deva where savannah was on his way from the northern region to the south on some business or other and he heard the verbal uttara teaching the monks this doctrine in dava jalika then just as a strong man might stretch forth his bent arm or might bend his outstretched arm even so the royal deva savannah disappeared from dava jalika and appeared among the devas of the 33 and v savannah approached sakha devaraja and said your grace i would have you know that this verbal uttara in [Music] teaches this doctrine well it is from time to time to review 1's own faults well it is to review another's well it is to review 1's own attainments well it is to review another's then as a strong man might bend and stretch out his arm again saka king of the devas disappeared from among the devas of the 33 and appeared before the verbal uttara in dava jalika and sakha the deva king approached the venerable uttara and saluted him and sat down at 1 side so seated he said is it true sir as they say that the venerable uttara teaches this doctrine to the monks namely well it is from time to time to review 1's own for to review anothers to review 1 owns attainments to reveal another's yes certainly they were king but pray sir is this the verbal uttara's own saying or is it the word of that exalted 1 arahan buddha now deva king i will give you an analogy for it is by analogy that men of intelligence understand the meaning of what he said imagine o king a great heap of grain near some village or market town from which country folk carry away corn on bingos or in baskets in lab or hand and if 1 should approach the fruit and question them saying from where i bring you this corn how would those folk in explaining best explain they would best explain the matter sir by saying we bring it from that great heap of grain even so o king whatsoever be well spoken all that is the word of the exalted 1 arahan buddha holy base thereon is both what me and others say it is marvelous it is wonderful sir how truly this has been said by the vulnerable uttara whatsoever be well spoken all that is the word of the exalted 1 buddha holy base thereon is what we and others say and then saka devaraja continued said once not long after the the departure of devadatta the exalted 1 was dwelling on vultures peak near raja gaha then the exalted 1 spoke thus to the monks concerning devadatta monks well it is for a monk to review from time to time is own false well it is for a monk to review from time to time another is false well it is for a month to review from time to time his own attainments relatives for among to review from time to time another's attainments monks mastered by 8 wrong states devadatta with his mind out of control became 1 doomed to suffer in hell in perdition dwelling there for a kalpa as a world cycle irreprievable by 1 aid mastered by gain by loss by fame by obscurity by honor by lack of honor by evil intentions by evil friendship with his mind out of control devadatta became 1 doomed to suffer in hell in perdition dwelling there a kappa irreprievable master by these data became irreprievable monks well it is that a monk should live mastering gain loss fame obscurity honor lack of honor evil intentions and evil friendship which arise and why and for what good purpose should a man live mastering gain loss and such states which arise nuns when a man lives with gain love's fame obscurity honor lack of honor evil intentions and evil friendship which arise unmastered there arise the ass of us full of distress and anguish but when those states are mastered those are full of distress and anguish are not for this good purpose monks a mom should master those states and so abide therefore monks train yourselves thus we will live mastering gain which arises mastering loss fame obscurity honor lack of honor evil intentions mastering evil friendship which arises train yourselves thus monks and and then the saka devaraja told the venable uttara worthy uttara this is a dhamma discourse nowhere honored among the 4 companies namely the monks nuns and lay disciples both men and women sir let the verbal uttara learn by heart this dhamma discourse let him master this dharma discourse let him bear it in mind this dharma discusser is charged with good it is the first principle of holy living as the end of the sutta
commentary: which is quite interesting because you see the monk is teaching dhamma and the devas they know and then when they know they discuss it among themselves and then saka devaraja came to talk with this tara which shows that the verbal uttara has some psychic ability here he can see the deva and talk to the deva and then this deva raja that the buddha spoke and he asked to teach the monks and nuns and lay disciples this suta because apparently they seem to have forgotten about it they don't practice this so so sakadeva raja himself you can see here is quite familiar with the suttas book of the chapter of the aids there's many important sutas in this book now i come to
aud_file: 070_Anguttara Nikaya Book 8A 812 - 8110 by Bhante Hye Dhammavuddho Mahathera.mp3

items: Well it is for a monk to review from time to time his own false
Well it is for a monk to review from time to time another's false
Well it is for a month to review from time to time his own attainments relatives
Well it is to review from time to time another's attainments
Monks well it is that a monk should live mastering gain loss fame obscurity honor lack of honor evil intentions and evil friendship which arise
When a man lives with gain love's fame obscurity honor lack of honor evil intentions and evil friendship which arise unmastered there arise the ass of us full of distress and anguish
But when those states are mastered those are full of distress and anguish are not for this good purpose
Therefore monks train yourselves thus we will live mastering gain which arises mastering loss fame obscurity honor lack of honor evil intentions mastering evil friendship which arises
